the number of pages read each day follows a quadratic sequence. the formula for the number of pages read on the n-th day is p(n) = 3n² - 3n + 5. this formula is derived from the given values: 5, 12, and 21. the sequence is quadratic, so the second difference is constant. the general formula is p(n) = 3n² - 3n + 5.  

the total number of pages read by the end of 30 days is calculated by summing the sequence from n=1 to n=30. using the formula p(n) = 3n² - 3n + 5, the sum is 3*(30² - 30 + 1) - 3*(30 - 1) + 5*30. this results in a total of 8700 pages. the relationship holds consistently throughout the 30-day period.
